title: Creating box plots
description: More practice making box plots to summarize data sets.
topic_path: Get ready for exploring one-variable quantitative data

assessment_items.question_content:
The data below represents the number of essays that students in Mr. Ji's class wrote.

$2$, $3$, $5$, $5$, $6$, $7$, $8$, $8$, $11$

**Which box plot correctly summarizes the data?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\begin{align}
& 2, {\large3},  {\large5}, 5,  \purpleD{6},  7, {\large8},  {\large8}, 11\\\\
& Q_{1} = \dfrac{3 + 5}{2} = 4\\\\
& Q_{3} = \dfrac{8 + 8}{2}= 8
\end{align}$

The following box plot correctly summarizes the data.

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A horizontal boxplot is plotted along a horizontal axis marked from 0 to 14, in increments of 1. A left whisker extends from 2 to 4. The box extends from 4 to 8 and is divided into 2 parts by a vertical line segment at 6. The right whisker extends from 8 to 11. All values estimated.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6f4accaa9ec8cecd1e045fe26709f92d.png)

![A horizontal boxplot is plotted along a horizontal axis marked from 0 to 14, in increments of 1. A left whisker extends from 2 to 3. The box extends from 3 to 7 and is divided into 2 parts by a vertical line segment at 6. The right whisker extends from 7 to 11. All values estimated.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e2628b3a1cc8700faf90f93178d3aeb4.png)

![A horizontal boxplot is plotted along a horizontal axis marked from 0 to 14, in increments of 1. A left whisker extends from 2 to 4. The box extends from 4 to 9 and is divided into 2 parts by a vertical line segment at 6. The right whisker extends from 9 to 12. All values estimated.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6a49a0e6d0a797e39c0fd817c579e82a.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
The data below represents the number of guests staying on each floor of the Luxed Hotel.

$1$, $3$, $5$, $7$, $9$, $9$, $11$, $11$, $13$, $14$

**Which box plot correctly summarizes the data?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$ 1$, $3$, $ {\large5}$, $7$, $9$![A series of 4 vertical dots represent the space between the 2 middle numbers.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1f30072a4a2224d05210d39afcd4eeac.png)$ 9$, $11$, $ {\large11}$, $13$, $14$

$\begin{align}
 Q_{1}  &= 5\\\\
 Q_{3} &= 11
\end{align}$

The following box plot correctly summarizes the data.

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A horizontal boxplot is plotted along a horizontal axis marked from 0 to 15, in increments of 1. A left whisker extends from 1 to 5. The box extends from 5 to 11 and is divided into 2 parts by a vertical line segment at 9. The right whisker extends from 11 to 14. All values estimated.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c04bf51a55250c9864f20ce16cf880fc.png)

![https://cdn.kastatic.org/ka-perseus-graphie/2fae05d5f5a75fed28ce785926daab8A horizontal boxplot is plotted along a horizontal axis marked from 0 to 15, in increments of 1. A left whisker extends from 1 to 3. The box extends from 3 to 11 and is divided into 2 parts by a vertical line segment at 9. The right whisker extends from 11 to 14. All values estimated.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/31d6a0455c950f79422041baa48cb474.png)

![A horizontal boxplot is plotted along a horizontal axis marked from 0 to 15, in increments of 1. A left whisker extends from 1 to 3. The box extends from 3 to 13 and is divided into 2 parts by a vertical line segment at 9. The right whisker extends from 13 to 14. All values estimated.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/72f8e643a2c9517cdd0741fc3b453cbb.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
Skiing is a snow sport where skiers glide over snowy hills.

The data below represents the number of practices each member of Jimenai's ski team attended.

$25$, $28$, $29$, $29$, $30$, $34$, $35$, $35$, $37$, $38$

**Which box plot correctly summarizes the data?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:

$\begin{align}
 Q_{1} &= 29\\\\
 Q_{3} &= 35
\end{align}$

The following box plot correctly summarizes the data.

[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
The data below represents the number of workouts each member of Big Muscles Gym attended last month.

$4$, $5$, $7$, $7$, $7$, $8$, $10$, $11$, $11$, $13$, $13$, $14$

**Which box plot correctly summarizes the data?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$ 4$, $5$, ${\large{7}}$, ${\large{7}}$, $7$, $8$![A series of 4 vertical dots represent the space between the 2 middle numbers.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1f30072a4a2224d05210d39afcd4eeac.png)$ 10$, $11$, ${\large{11}}$, ${\large{13}}$, $13$, $14$

$\begin{align}
 Q_{1}  &= \dfrac{{7} + {7}}{2}&=7\\\\
 Q_{3} &= \dfrac{{11} + {13}}{2}&= 12
\end{align}$

The following box plot correctly summarizes the data.

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A horizontal boxplot is plotted along a horizontal axis marked from 0 to 16, in increments of 1. A left whisker extends from 4 to 7. The box extends from 7 to 12 and is divided into 2 parts by a vertical line segment at 9. The right whisker extends from 12 to 14. All values estimated.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5e2b9bf6ce161a6a48e4d04de0cff1d4.png)

![A horizontal boxplot is plotted along a horizontal axis marked from 0 to 16, in increments of 1. A left whisker extends from 2 to 7. The box extends from 7 to 13 and is divided into 2 parts by a vertical line segment at 9. The right whisker extends from 13 to 14. All values estimated.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c86de0aeca0b3ee6051682c96db9f754.png)

![A horizontal boxplot is plotted along a horizontal axis marked from 0 to 16, in increments of 1. A left whisker extends from 4 to 7. The box extends from 7 to 13 and is divided into 2 parts by a vertical line segment at 9. The right whisker extends from 13 to 14. All values estimated.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/00fd52ba32c45038a1c42b18d8d1085c.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
The data below represents the number of pages each student in Ashwin's class read during reading time.

$16$, $16$, $16$, $20$, $21$, $21$, $23$, $25$, $26$, $26$, $28$, $28$

**Which box plot correctly summarizes the data?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$ 16$, $16$, ${\large{16}}$, ${\large{20}}$, $21$, $21$![A series of 4 vertical dots represent the space between the 2 middle numbers.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1f30072a4a2224d05210d39afcd4eeac.png)$ 23$, $25$, ${\large{26}}$, ${\large{26}}$, $28$, $28$

$\begin{align}
 Q_{1} &= \dfrac{{16} + {20}}{2} &= 18\\\\
 Q_{3} &=\dfrac{{26} + {26}}{2}&= 26
\end{align}$

The following box plot correctly summarizes the data.

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A horizontal boxplot is plotted along a horizontal axis marked from 15 to 29, in increments of 1. A left whisker extends from 16 to 18. The box extends from 18 to 26 and is divided into 2 parts by a vertical line segment at 22. The right whisker extends from 26 to 28. All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1230262c3d1a570c9f757acc37a0d25bcafcd800)

![A horizontal boxplot is plotted along a horizontal axis marked from 15 to 29, in increments of 1. A left whisker extends from 16 to 18. The box extends from 18 to 25 and is divided into 2 parts by a vertical line segment at 21. The right whisker extends from 25 to 28. All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bc97b4c16f3fd1326000723c6712e1f3eaa212a4)

![A horizontal boxplot is plotted along a horizontal axis marked from 15 to 29, in increments of 1. A left whisker extends from 16 to 18. The box extends from 18 to 26 and is divided into 2 parts by a vertical line segment at 23. The right whisker extends from 26 to 28. All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e34979d663e8c56487be3ba95a43db67664b9238)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which data set could be represented by the box plot shown below?**

![A horizontal boxplot is plotted along a horizontal axis marked from 0 to 15, in increments of 1. A left whisker extends from 2 to 4. The box extends from 4 to 8 and is divided into 2 parts by a vertical line segment at 6. The right whisker extends from 8 to 11. All values estimated.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c2a766913ddac2607e194ea472d83f84.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: [[☃ image 1]]

The minimum of the box plot is $2$.

We can eliminate one of the data sets because it does not have a minimum of $2$.

$ \redE{\large3}$, $3$, $5$, $5$, $6$, $7$, $8$, $9$, $11$

The median of the box plot is $6$.

We can eliminate one of the data sets because it does not have a median of $6$.

$ 2$, $3$, $5$, $5$, $\redE{\large7}$, $7$, $8$, $10$, $11$

The third quartile of the box plot is $8$.

We can eliminate one of the data sets because it does not have a third quartile of $8$.

$ 2$, $3$, $5$, $5$, $6$, $7$, $\redE{\large8}$, $\redE{\large{10}}$, $11$

The following data set  could be represented by the box plot.

$\maroonD{\large{2}}$, ${\large{3}}$, ${\large{5}}$, $5$, $\purpleD{\large{6}}$, $7$, ${\large{8}}$, ${\large{8}}$, $\blueE{\large{11}}$

[[☃ image 2]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which data set could be represented by the box plot shown below?**

![A horizontal boxplot is plotted along a horizontal axis marked from 20 to 36, in increments of 1. A left whisker extends from 24 to 27. The box extends from 27 to 33 and is divided into 2 parts by a vertical line segment at 31. The right whisker extends from 33 to 34. We can eliminate two of the data sets because they do not have a median of $31$.

hints: [[☃ image 1]]
